title: [Feature]: Evaluate an Oracle TimesTen sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists Oracle TimesTen. nats-sinks does not currently define a TimesTen-specific sink strategy, durability boundary, or idempotency pattern.
proposal: Research whether Oracle TimesTen should be supported through the existing Oracle Database sink, an ODBC/JDBC-style connector, or a dedicated first-party Oracle TimesTen sink. The design should evaluate transaction semantics, connection drivers, table DDL, idempotent upsert support, and whether in-memory deployment modes satisfy critical custody requirements.
users: Oracle TimesTen users, low-latency application teams, and operators considering NATS-to-TimesTen event persistence.
delivery_semantics: The sink may return success only after a TimesTen transaction has committed under a documented durable mode. Volatile or cache-only modes must be marked unsafe for ACK-gated processing.
security: Connection strings, credentials, table names, and payloads must be redacted. Identifier validation and bind variables are mandatory.
acceptance: Research selects a supported Python integration approach.
Durable and non-durable TimesTen modes are documented separately.
Idempotent write modes are specified before implementation.
Unit tests cover SQL generation or client behavior with fakes.
Documentation warns when deployment mode cannot provide durable custody.
tests: Add mocked driver tests for config validation, SQL binding, duplicate handling, and error translation. Optional live TimesTen tests stay gated.
documentation: Add TimesTen evaluation notes and roadmap entries.
closeout: Close after release of the research decision or implementation.
